title: ERP Finance HR Marketing Opportunity Map
summary: Provides a cross-functional Business Value map across ERP, finance, HR, marketing, growth, and leadership so Joz can explain AI opportunities in operational terms.
body:
The most practical Business Value extension is a cross-functional opportunity map.

ERP and operations
- automate exception handling
- summarize demand and supply issues
- improve procurement visibility
- surface bottlenecks in fulfillment and planning
- support vendor and supplier decision-making

Finance and accounting
- accelerate AP and AR workflows
- classify and reconcile invoices
- detect anomalies and leakage
- improve spend visibility
- support close, forecast, and margin review

HR and workforce
- improve recruiting triage
- support onboarding and internal helpdesk workflows
- make policy and process knowledge easier to use
- accelerate training and knowledge reuse
- support managers with better internal context

Marketing
- accelerate campaign planning and reporting
- improve segmentation and content operations
- turn noisy analytics into clearer decisions
- support CAC efficiency and conversion analysis

Growth and leadership
- improve cross-functional reporting
- reduce decision latency
- create better next-best-action logic
- improve management leverage through trusted intelligence
- support scalable adoption of AI into real workflows

This gives Joz a practical way to talk about business value in terms leaders and operators can act on immediately.

claims: A cross-functional opportunity map is the most practical way to extend Joz for Business Value.
ERP, finance, HR, marketing, and leadership each have distinct AI opportunity areas with direct operational relevance.
Joz should talk about AI opportunities in terms leaders and operators can act on immediately.
proof_points: This structure is consistent with the existing business-need and decision-intelligence direction in the corpus.
The map converts abstract AI language into function-level business opportunities.
It aligns with Joz's workflow, operating-model, and enterprise-transformation positioning.
